title: Amauropelta resinifera

More specifically, it belongs to the genus Amauropelta, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical regions. intro: Amauropelta resinifera, also known as the resin fern or resinous Amauropelta, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. The resin fern has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it a valuable plant for overall health and well-being. The bioactive constituents of Amauropelta resinifera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the resin fern is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of capsules, tinctures, and essential oils to harness its health benefits.
